meaning: vt.赶上,追上,(暴风雨、麻烦等)突然来袭,压倒
examples: It's dangerous to overtake on a bend.
Coming out of the final bend, the runner stepped up a gear to overtake the rest of the pack.
Come, waste not the time—cannot I overtake him?
If current trends are any indication, mobile revenues will overtake total fixed-line revenues by 2004.
It is already the world's second-biggest economy, and in time is likely to overtake America.
